Security levels 1–5 depending on parameters. Stateful hash-based (HSS/LMS); CNSA 2.0 required for firmware signing. W8 Winternitz parameter gives smallest signatures. State management critical — private key state must never be reused.

Family entry — Leighton-Micali Signatures (RFC 8554, NIST SP 800-208). STATEFUL hash-based signature; private-key state must NEVER be reused or signatures forge. CNSA 2.0 approved for firmware/code signing only. Public key 64 B for all parameter sets; signature 1400–6200 B (range across H=5 to H=25 tree heights, varies by Winternitz parameter). For concrete parameter-set sizes & performance see "LMS-SHA256 (H20/W8)" elsewhere in this CSV. Verified against pqctoday-priv/docs/PQC_Algorithm_Reference_April2026.csv (NIST SP 800-208 / RFC 8554).

Family entry — eXtended Merkle Signature Scheme (RFC 8391, NIST SP 800-208). STATEFUL hash-based; same state-reuse forge risk as LMS. CNSA 2.0 approved for firmware/code signing. Public key 64–128 B; private key ~2–4 KB; signature 2,500–9,092 B (across XMSS / XMSS^MT variants, SHA-256 or SHAKE256). For concrete parameter-set sizes & performance see "XMSS-SHA2_20" elsewhere in this CSV. Verified against pqctoday-priv/docs/PQC_Algorithm_Reference_April2026.csv (NIST SP 800-208 / RFC 8391).

fips_standard: BSI TR-02102-1 (recommended); NOT in NIST FIPS
use_case_notes: Family entry — binary Goppa code-based KEM. NOT selected by NIST FIPS (impractical key sizes — 256 KB to 1.3 MB public keys). NIST dropped Classic McEliece from active standardisation in 2024, but it remains BSI-recommended for sovereign long-term protection of classified data. Sizes are ranges across L1 (Classic-McEliece-348864: pk=261120 / sk=6492 / ct=128) to L5 (Classic-McEliece-8192128: pk=1357824 / sk=14120 / ct=208). For concrete parameter-set rows see "Classic-McEliece-348864", "-460896" (L3), "-6688128" (L5), "-8192128" (L5) elsewhere in this CSV. Verified against pqctoday-priv/docs/PQC_Algorithm_Reference_April2026.csv.

Family entry — Hamming Quasi-Cyclic code-based KEM. NIST-selected March 2025 as the 5th PQC algorithm (code-based backup vs. lattice ML-KEM). DRAFT FIPS spec in progress; spec may change before final standard. Sizes are ranges across L1/L3/L5 parameter sets (HQC-128: pk=2249/sk=2289/ct=4497; HQC-256: pk=7245/sk=7285/ct=14485). For concrete parameter-set rows see "HQC-128/-192/-256" elsewhere in this CSV. Verified against pqctoday-priv/docs/PQC_Algorithm_Reference_April2026.csv (HQC Spec 2025-08-22).

fips_standard: NIST Additional Sig — Round 2 candidate (NOT standardised)
use_case_notes: NIST Additional Sig Round 2 candidate — Multivariate (Unbalanced Oil and Vinegar). Sizes shown are for UOV-Ip (NIST L1 reference). Public key is ~66 KB raw, but compressed PK (~1.2 KB) is strongly recommended for deployment. Private key ~237 KB. Tiny 96 B signature. Other parameter sets exist (e.g., UOV-IIIp: pk=265 KB raw / sk=1.1 MB / sig=148 B). Performance not yet benchmarked for this CSV. Final NIST selection ~2027. Verified against pqctoday-priv/docs/PQC_Algorithm_Reference_April2026.csv (NIST CSRC Round 2).

fips_standard: NIST Additional Sig — Round 2 candidate (NOT standardised)
use_case_notes: NIST Additional Sig Round 2 candidate — Isogeny-based (supersingular quaternion algebras). pk=64 / sk=782 / sig=177 from SQIsign-I (NIST L1) per SQIsign v2.0 spec. SMALLEST combined PK+sig of any PQC candidate. Signing is known to be very slow (cycle benchmarks not yet recorded here). Other parameter sets: SQIsign-III (L3): pk=96/sk=1152/sig=263; SQIsign-V (L5): pk=128/sk=1512/sig=335. Final NIST selection ~2027. Verified against pqctoday-priv/docs/PQC_Algorithm_Reference_April2026.csv (SQIsign v2.0 spec).

fips_standard: NIST Additional Sig — Round 2 candidate (NOT standardised)
use_case_notes: NIST Additional Sig Round 2 candidate — Code-based, MPC-in-the-head paradigm (Codes and Restricted Objects Signature Scheme). Sizes shown are for CROSS-R-SDP-1b (NIST L1, Cloudflare-benchmarked variant): pk=54 B (tiny!), sig=12,872 B. Other variants exist with different size/perf tradeoffs (e.g., CROSS-R-SDP-3b: pk=59/sig=23380 — Sign≈2.6M cycles, Verify≈1.5M). Cycle counts are absolute Skylake measurements from Cloudflare blog, not relative to ML-KEM-512 baseline used elsewhere in this CSV. Final NIST selection ~2027. Verified against pqctoday-priv/docs/PQC_Algorithm_Reference_April2026.csv (Cloudflare / NIST CSRC Round 2).

fips_standard: NIST Additional Sig — Round 2 candidate (NOT standardised)
use_case_notes: NIST Additional Sig Round 2 candidate — Code-based (Linear Equivalence Signature Scheme). Sizes shown are for LESS-1b (NIST L1 balanced): pk=13,940 B, sig=9,286 B. Other parameter sets: LESS-3b (NIST L3 balanced): pk=35,074 B, sig=18,000 B. Cycle counts not published under standard methodology — performance not yet benchmarked here. Final NIST selection ~2027. Verified against pqctoday-priv/docs/PQC_Algorithm_Reference_April2026.csv (NIST CSRC Round 2).

fips_standard: NIST Additional Sig — Round 2 candidate (NOT standardised)
use_case_notes: NIST Additional Sig Round 2 candidate — Symmetric (Fast AES-based with Tight security; VOLE-in-the-head paradigm). SMALLEST PK and SK of all NIST PQC candidates: pk=sk=32 B. Sizes shown are for FAEST-128f (NIST L1 fast variant): sig=6,336 B. Other variants: FAEST-128s (small-sig, sig=5,688 B), FAEST-192f (L3, sig=16,792 B), FAEST-256f (L5, sig=28,400 B). Security reduces to AES — large signatures the trade-off. Performance not yet benchmarked here. Final NIST selection ~2027. Verified against pqctoday-priv/docs/PQC_Algorithm_Reference_April2026.csv (FAEST Round 1 spec).

fips_standard: NIST Additional Sig — Round 2 candidate (NOT standardised)
use_case_notes: NIST Additional Sig Round 2 candidate — Multivariate (Simple Noncommutative-ring-based UOV variant). Sizes shown are for SNOVA l=4 (NIST L1): pk=1,016 B, sk=48 B, sig=248 B — competitive with ML-DSA in TLS. Other parameter sets exist (e.g., SNOVA l=5 (L3): pk=2,415/sk=64/sig=376). Round 2 introduced new l=5 parameter sets — verify exact IDs against current spec. Performance not yet benchmarked here. Final NIST selection ~2027. Verified against pqctoday-priv/docs/PQC_Algorithm_Reference_April2026.csv (SNOVA Round 2 spec).

Formal IETF identifier: id-MLDSA44-ECDSA-P256-SHA256 (formerly listed in this CSV as "ML-DSA-44-ECDSA-P256"). IETF Internet-Draft (draft-ietf-lamps-pq-composite-sigs); NOT yet an RFC. Two-component AND-construction (both ML-DSA-44 and ECDSA P-256 must verify). Companion CSV entries exist for Ed25519 and RSA-2048-PSS variants of ML-DSA-44. Concrete encoded composite sizes for the ECDSA-P256 specifically are NOT confirmed in the reference document — expect a similar magnitude to the Ed25519 variant (PQ component dominates: ML-DSA-44 pk=1312 / sig=2420; ECDSA-P256 contributes pk≈64–65 / sig≈64–72) plus ASN.1/OID overhead. Refer to the active draft tables for authoritative wire sizes.

Formal IETF identifier: id-MLDSA44-Ed25519-SHA512 (formerly listed in this CSV as "ML-DSA-44-Ed25519"). IETF Internet-Draft (draft-ietf-lamps-pq-composite-sigs); NOT yet an RFC. Two-component AND-construction with Ed25519 classical companion. pk = 1312+32 = 1344 B (PQ + classical components). sig = 2420+64 = 2484 B (PQ + classical components, before ASN.1/OID encoding overhead which adds ~10–30 B). Verified against pqctoday-priv/docs/PQC_Algorithm_Reference_April2026.csv (draft-ietf-lamps-pq-composite-sigs).

Formal IETF identifier: id-MLDSA44-RSA2048-PSS-SHA256 (formerly listed in this CSV as "ML-DSA-44-RSA2048-PSS"). IETF Internet-Draft (draft-ietf-lamps-pq-composite-sigs); NOT yet an RFC. Two-component AND-construction with RSA-2048 PSS classical companion. pk = 1312+256 = 1568 B (PQ + classical components). sig = 2420+256 = 2676 B (PQ + classical components, before ASN.1/OID encoding overhead). Verified against pqctoday-priv/docs/PQC_Algorithm_Reference_April2026.csv (draft-ietf-lamps-pq-composite-sigs).

IETF Internet-Draft (draft-ietf-tls-ecdhe-mlkem); NOT yet an RFC, but actively used as the FIPS-compliant TLS hybrid NamedGroup. KEM combiner derives shared secret from both ML-KEM-768 and ECDH P-256. pk = 1184+65 = 1249 B (PQ + classical components). ct = 1088+65 = 1153 B (PQ + classical components). Verified against pqctoday-priv/docs/PQC_Algorithm_Reference_April2026.csv (IETF TLS WG draft).

IETF Internet-Draft (draft-ietf-lamps-pq-composite-kem); NOT yet an RFC. KEM combiner with RSA-OAEP-2048 classical component. NOTE: this specific RSA-OAEP-2048 hybrid is NOT listed in the curated reference document (pqctoday-priv/docs/PQC_Algorithm_Reference_April2026.csv) — that reference covers the more widely-adopted X25519+ML-KEM-768 and P-256+ML-KEM-768 TLS hybrids instead. Concrete encoded composite sizes for the RSA-OAEP-2048 variant are NOT yet confirmed here; refer to the active draft-ietf-lamps-pq-composite-kem document for authoritative tables.

fips_standard: BSI TR-02102-1 (recommended Level 5); NOT in NIST FIPS
use_case_notes: Classic McEliece Level 5 parameter set (mceliece6688128). pk=1,044,992 / sk=13,932 / ct=208 per pqctoday-priv/docs/PQC_Algorithm_Reference_April2026.csv (sizes confirmed via OQS reference implementation). BSI-recommended for sovereign long-term protection of classified data; NOT in NIST FIPS (NIST dropped Classic McEliece from active standardisation in 2024). Distinct from Classic-McEliece-8192128 (also Level 5 — larger pk=1,357,824). An alternate L5 parameter set Classic-McEliece-6960119 also exists (pk=1,047,319 / sk=13,948 / ct=194). Cycle benchmarks not yet recorded here.

fips_standard: CACR Level 1 (CACR 1st Prize 2020) — referenced in NGCC track
use_case_notes: CAVEAT — Information UNCONFIRMED. LAC was a NIST Round 2 KEM candidate (Lu, Liu, Zhang 2017, Chinese-origin LWE KEM) and was ELIMINATED in NIST Round 2 — not advanced. Superseded by ML-KEM. DO NOT DEPLOY for any production purpose. Some sources estimate sizes ~352 B (pk and ct), but per pqctoday-priv/docs/PQC_Algorithm_Reference_April2026.csv these values are unconfirmed. Listed in the transitions CSV as a CACR (Chinese) submission referenced in the NGCC standardisation track (~2028); current authoritative spec and CACR provenance not yet researched. Entry exists primarily to satisfy cross-references — see NIST IR 8240 for the original Round 1 elimination context.
status: ELIMINATED — NIST Round 2 (not advanced); DO NOT DEPLOY

use_case_notes: ETSI-standardised hybrid attribute-based KEM with access control (KEMAC). Combines a classical KEM (ECIES) with a post-quantum KEM (ML-KEM) and enforces attribute-based access policies at the key layer. Sizes are inherently VARIABLE / policy-dependent — they scale with the complexity of the access policy, not just the KEM parameter set. The reference document (pqctoday-priv/docs/PQC_Algorithm_Reference_April2026.csv) confirms this and does not give concrete byte counts. Active deployments use Cosmian's reference implementation. ETSI TS 104 015 published Feb 2025.

"HPKE-PQ" is NOT a single concrete algorithm — it is HPKE (RFC 9180) configured with a PQ KEM. Sizes shown are for the most common mode HPKE-PQ (ML-KEM-768): pk=1184 B, ct=1088 B, shared secret=32 B. The HPKE framework itself wraps a KEM, a KDF, and an AEAD; concrete sizes vary entirely with the chosen KEM. IETF Internet-Draft draft-ietf-hpke-pq-03 (active, expires May 2026). Used by MLS messaging, Encrypted Client Hello (ECH), and Oblivious HTTP. Verified against pqctoday-priv/docs/PQC_Algorithm_Reference_April2026.csv.
status: IETF Internet-Draft (alias for HPKE with PQ KEM)

fips_standard: BSI/conservative national security recs; NOT in NIST FIPS
use_case_notes: Classic McEliece alternate Level 5 parameter set (mceliece6960119). pk=1,047,319 / sk=13,948 / ct=194 per pqctoday-priv/docs/PQC_Algorithm_Reference_April2026.csv (sizes confirmed via OQS reference implementation). BSI-recommended (alongside -6688128) as a conservative ~256-bit-equivalent option for long-term sovereign protection. Distinct from -6688128 (pk=1,044,992, ct=208) and -8192128 (pk=1,357,824, ct=208) — same security level, different code parameters. NIST dropped Classic McEliece from active standardisation in 2024. Cycle benchmarks not yet recorded here.
